title: Bipolar & Lithium Mastery
content:
• Dx: Manic Episode: Sleeping little for 8 days, pressured speech, risky behavior. Bipolar I if cycled with depression.
• Risk: Lithium Toxicity: Precipitated by NSAIDs (Advil). Better pain meds are Aspirin or Sulindac.
• Symptoms: N/V/D, coarse tremor, ataxia, confusion, slurred speech.
• Dx: EKG: T-wave flattening or inversion + U waves.
• Tx: Fluid resuscitation; Emergent dialysis if Level >4 or kidney disease.
• Therapeutic levels: 0.6-1.2. Monitoring: Li level q4-8wks, TFTs q6mo, Cr.

title: Personality Clusters Mnemonics
content:
• Cluster A (Weird): Paranoid (Accusatory/Distrust), Schizoid (Aloof/Detached), Schizotypal (Awkward/Eccentric).
• Cluster B (Wild): Antisocial (Bad/Disregard), Borderline (Instability), Histrionic (flamBoyant/Attention-seeking), Narcissistic (Best/Grandiosity).
• Cluster C (Worried): Avoidant (Cowardly), OCPD (Compulsive/Orderly), Dependent (Clingy/Submissive).

title: Childhood Tics & Disorders
content:
• Dx: Tourette’s: Tics occur at least once a day for 1 year without a gap longer than 3 months.
• Tx: First line? Clonidine (benign profile). Most effective? Haloperidol or Pimozide.
• Dx: Separation Anxiety: Abdominal pain only on school days, not on weekends.
• Dx: Rumination Disorder: Regurgitating and re-chewing food. Check lead levels.

title: APGAR & Birth Injuries
content:
• Dx: APGAR: Tells labor tolerance (1 min) and resuscitation response (5 min). Does NOT predict neurologic outcome.
• Dx: Erb-Duchenne (C5-C6): Right arm medially rotated (Waiters tip). Refer if not better by 3-6mo.
• Dx: Caput Succedaneum: Edema that crosses suture lines.
• Dx: Cephalohematoma: Fluctuance that does NOT cross suture lines.
• Tx: Clavicular Fracture: No treatment needed; will form callus in 1 week.

title: Congenital Heart Defects
content:
• Dx: TGA: Most common in infants of diabetic mothers. Cyanotic at birth, O2 does not help.
• Dx: TOF: Cyanotic during play, squatting helps. SEM murmur + single S2.
• Dx: VSD: Most common heart defect. Harsh holosystolic murmur at LLSB.
• Dx: PDA: Continuous machine-like murmur. Tx: Indomethacin.

title: Pediatric GI Emergencies
content:
• Dx: Pyloric Stenosis: 4-week-old, projectile non-bilious vomiting, olive-shaped mass. Tx: Hydration and Pyloromyotomy.
• Dx: Intussusception: Colicky pain, currant jelly stool, sausage-shaped mass. Tx: Air or Barium enema (diagnostic & therapeutic).
• Dx: Duodenal Atresia: Associated with Down Syndrome; 'Double-bubble' on X-ray.

title: Consent, Capacity & SDM
content:
• Capacity Assessment: Check understanding, appreciation, reasoning, and choice.
• Risk: Adult Refusal: Do not override a capable adult even if treatment is life-saving (e.g., Jehovah’s Witness).
• Rule: Mature Minor: Consent is capacity-based, not age-based. Contraception/Abortion can be confidential.

title: Mandatory Reporting in Canada
content:
• Risk: Child Abuse: Report immediately to child protection on reasonable suspicion; do not investigate alone.
• Risk: Fitness to Drive: Counsel to stop and report per provincial mandate (e.g., uncontrolled seizures).
• Rule: Error Disclosure: Disclose promptly even if no harm occurred; apologize and document.

title: Breaking Confidentiality & Privacy
content:
• Exceptions: Imminent risk to self/others, child protection, public health diseases, court order.
• Rule: Media requests: Decline patient-specific info; de-identify thoroughly if speaking.
• Rule: Prisoners: Ensure privacy; ask guards to step out unless there is a safety risk.
